title: WordPress REST API — Taxonomies & Post Types

actors.name: API Consumer
actors.description: Any unauthenticated or authenticated HTTP client querying metadata endpoints

actors.name: Authenticated Editor
actors.description: Authenticated user with edit_posts or assign_terms capabilities; unlocks 'edit' context fields

actors.name: WordPress Core
actors.description: System actor providing get_taxonomies(), get_post_types(), get_post_stati() registry data

use_cases.name: List Taxonomies
use_cases.description: Returns all registered taxonomies with show_in_rest=true. Optional ?type= filter narrows to taxonomies of a specific post type.
use_cases.permission_rules: Always public. Edit context requires current_user_can(assign_terms) on at least one taxonomy.

use_cases.name: Retrieve Single Taxonomy
use_cases.description: Returns metadata for a single taxonomy by slug. Returns 404 if not found or show_in_rest=false.
use_cases.permission_rules: Always public. Edit context requires current_user_can(assign_terms). Returns false (403) if taxonomy has show_in_rest=false.

use_cases.name: Filter Taxonomies by Post Type
use_cases.description: Extends UC1: filters returned taxonomies to those associated with a given post type slug.

use_cases.name: List Post Types
use_cases.description: Returns all post types with show_in_rest=true.
use_cases.permission_rules: Always public. Edit context requires current_user_can(edit_posts) on at least one post type.

use_cases.name: Retrieve Single Post Type
use_cases.description: Returns metadata for a specific post type. Returns 404 if not found, 403 if show_in_rest=false, or 403 on edit context without edit_posts capability.
use_cases.permission_rules: Permission callback is __return_true; visibility enforced inside get_item(). Edit context requires current_user_can(edit_posts).

use_cases.name: List Post Statuses
use_cases.description: Returns all non-internal post statuses plus 'trash'. Each is filtered through check_read_permission().
use_cases.permission_rules: Public statuses always returned. Non-public (non-internal or trash) require current_user_can(edit_posts). Edit context requires edit_posts.

use_cases.name: Retrieve Single Post Status
use_cases.description: Returns metadata for a specific post status. Returns 404 if not found, 403 if not readable.
use_cases.permission_rules: Uses check_read_permission(): public=true always allowed; internal=false or trash requires edit_posts on any REST-enabled post type.

shared_behaviors.name: Context Parameter
shared_behaviors.description: All endpoints accept ?context= (view|edit|embed). 'edit' returns extra fields (capabilities, labels, visibility). Enforced via filter_response_by_context().

shared_behaviors.name: JSON Schema Response
shared_behaviors.description: All controllers define get_item_schema() returning JSON Schema draft-04 objects. Exposed via GET schema endpoint.

shared_behaviors.name: HAL Links
shared_behaviors.description: Responses include _links: collection (back to list) and https://api.w.org/items (link to the actual items endpoint). Statuses link to archives (filtered posts list).

shared_behaviors.name: Filter Hook
shared_behaviors.description: Each controller applies a filter before returning: rest_prepare_taxonomy, rest_prepare_post_type, rest_prepare_status.

authorization_matrix.required_cap: assign_terms (on at least one REST-enabled taxonomy)
edit_posts (on at least one REST-enabled post type)

authorization_matrix.note: Only public statuses returned to anonymous; non-public need edit_posts

authorization_matrix.required_cap: edit_posts (on at least one REST-enabled post type)

authorization_matrix.note: Must have show_in_rest=true; edit context still requires edit_posts

important_notes: All controllers extend WP_REST_Controller and use namespace 'wp/v2'. These are read-only (GET-only) endpoints — no create/update/delete. WP_REST_Post_Types_Controller get_item() uses __return_true as permission_callback, enforcing visibility rules inside the method body instead. WP_REST_Post_Statuses_Controller has a protected check_read_permission() helper used by both get_items() and get_item_permissions_check(). Since WordPress 5.9, method parameters were renamed for PHP 8 named parameter compatibility. Post type responses include associated taxonomy slugs via get_object_taxonomies().
